items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, leading to remote code execution. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a full-page cache extension, allows remote code execution via a crafted CacheWarmer cookie. This critical flaw is actively exploited in the wild.

items.title: Adobe Security Bulletin APSB26-49 (Multiple Vulnerabilities)
items.summary: This security bulletin resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, including arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass. CVE-2026-34646, an Incorrect Authorization vulnerability, allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access.

items.title: Magento LTS Auth Bypass Vulnerability
items.summary: An authentication bypass flaw in OpenMage Magento Long Term Support (LTS) allows attackers to hijack API sessions through predictable session IDs due to a cryptographic weakness in the session ID generator.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (APSB26-05)
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Successful exploitation could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, gain unauthorized access to store data, and compromise customer accounts.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical flaw in Magento's REST API allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store by using a polyglot to bypass validation. Sansec has observed widespread attacks exploiting this vulnerability.
